ref: In the crowd was a woman who had been bleeding for twelve years.
text: Nya usio utsyuǝ akɛ lǝ mǝ nti, wǝ kɔtɔ díkakpo lǝ lefosi alɛ ǝnuǝ sɔ̃ɔ̃ ǝsuǝ.
translated: The woman who is not a witch, she is the one who has the power to heal and bring people together.

ref: When the disciples heard this, they were greatly surprised and asked, “How can anyone ever be saved?”
text: Lefe nǝ bakasebi bamǝ lɛnɔ nyamfoo, íyifo mǝ yanii, nyaso bátɔ bǝnkǝ, “Nyalɛ owoe ko luunyǝ nkpǝ manlo?”
translated: When the old men were gathered at the foot of the baobab tree, one of them asked, "Who has ever seen a leopard's buttocks?"

ref: Once again Jesus got into the boat and crossed Lake Galilee. Then as he stood on the shore, a large crowd gathered around him.
text: Yesu ǝsiǝ lǝ ɔklɔ úsinkli úsu ditumbukplɛ nǝmǝ eto Yudafɔ eto lekpake nǝ nse. Se úsu údufǝ ditumbukplɛ nǝmǝ eto kɛnyɛ mfóo, betidi kpǝ ǝbǝ bátsyilama wǝ.
translated: And Jesus went into the temple of God, and cast out all them that sold and bought in the temple, and overthrew the tables of the moneychangers, and the seats of them that sold doves, and he said unto them

ref: Then his disciples came over to him and asked, “Do you know you insulted the Pharisees by what you said?”
text: Nya wǝ bakasebi díbǝ wǝ ɔflɔ bátɛyi wǝ bǝnkǝ, “Eyi fǝnkǝ se Farisifɔ lɛnɔ asa nnya fátɛyii, éla mǝ kɛnyɛ lele tinti?”
translated: And they came to Jesus and found him sleeping, and they said to him, “Isn't this the son of the carpenter, how can he talk like that?”

ref: He took the twelve-year-old girl by the hand and said, “Talitha, koum!” which means, “Little girl, get up!” The girl got right up and started walking around. Everyone was greatly surprised.
text: Lamfolamfo, disiobi nǝmǝ nǝ dikafo lefosi alɛ ǝnuǝǝ, átaka úńyǝ nya útsyiko bɔyɛ katsyilama. Se íbǝ nkoo, íyifo mǝmblɛ yanii.
translated: The lazy person, because he is lazy, doesn't want to go and work, so he stays at home and sleeps, and when he wakes up, his colleagues have already eaten.

ref: But he didn't have any money to pay what he owed. The king ordered him to be sold, along with his wife and children and all he owned, in order to pay the debt.
text: Kulesa ente wǝ wuufi ofo kayo kamǝ esoo, oka ǝmǝ ǝtǝ nkǝ bate wǝ ku wuusiǝ ku wǝ bebi ku lesa saa nǝ nte wǝ lǝ befi bafo kayo kamǝ.
translated: When the moon is full, the women gather at the river bank to wash their clothes and the men go to the farm to harvest the crops.

ref: The field is the world, and the good seeds are the people who belong to the kingdom. The weeds are those who belong to the evil one,
text: Ɔkuɛ ǝmǝ ni kawunsiǝ. Nya eyibibi sɛɛ nyamǝ ni mba nkpe lǝ sekakedikɔ siamǝ. Sefa siamǝ ni betidi ba ni olontidi ǝmǝ eto bale.
translated: The dog is very fierce. When it sees a stranger, it will bite and not let go until it has torn the person's cloth. If it bites, it will not let go until it has swallowed the person's flesh.

ref: Go only to the people of Israel, because they are like a flock of lost sheep.
text: Bomu bisu Israel bebi ba diyifo fe basamu ba lénya kusu ɔflɔ.
translated: The people of Israel were in the wilderness for forty years.

ref: Jesus told the chief priests and leaders to listen to this story: A land owner once planted a vineyard. He built a wall around it and dug a pit to crush the grapes in. He also built a lookout tower. Then he rented out his vineyard and left the country.
text: “Bɛnɔ lekpa bamba nǝmfo. Ɔkuɛmfo utsyuǝ ǝsiǝ wǝ léfo eyibibi eto ɔkuɛ, nya ókpe ɔkpɔ otsyilama wǝ. Óto kaka beeke eyibibi eto nte kǝlǝkǝ nya óto nwǝ laase ɔkuɛ ǝmǝ eto kǝsiǝkɔ. Nya úfi ɔkuɛ ǝmǝ ókpe beyifoyifo bǝtsyuǝ lǝ ani nkǝ bakase wǝ, nya údu úsu kusu.
translated: When the elephant goes to drink water, the buffalo also goes to drink water, and if it is a small river, it drinks and goes back home. If it is a big river, it crosses to the other side and drinks and then comes back. And if the elephant is drinking at the riverside, the buffalo waits until the elephant finishes drinking before it drinks, and then it goes home.

ref: He sent other servants to say to the guests, “The banquet is ready! My cattle and prize calves have all been prepared. Everything is ready. Come to the banquet!”
text: “Nyaso óla wǝ beyifoyifo bamba ókpe nkǝ batɛyi betidi ba ókla utsyǝ nkǝ, ‘Lesalidife nǝmǝ eboyo! Bálo mɔ banantsyui ku banatsyui eto akpɔ nnya nkpe ayinkua bufi bɔlɛ mi kama bobe. Nya búkalɔ lesa saa bútsyǝ. Nyaso bibǝ kusiǝ komǝ kakpakɔ!’
translated: The hunters went to hunt in the bush, and when they got there, they said, 'Let's go and find something to eat! We have been searching for game all day, but we haven't caught anything, not even a grasshopper. We are very hungry. The hunters then decided to go home!

ref: When Peter had gone out to the gate, another servant girl saw him and said to some people there, “This man was with Jesus from Nazareth.”
text: Nya údu mfó úsu úńyǝ lǝ diyo nǝmǝ eto kesie. Usumubisiobi bamba tsya ǝnyǝ Petro nya ɔ́tɛyi mba díńyǝ mfó nkǝ, “Wuntsya ǝtǝkǝko Yesu Nasaretnyǝ ǝmǝ.”
translated: And it happened that a certain man named Peter went to a place, and he said to them, “In the name of Jesus of Nazareth, walk.”

ref: When the disciples heard the voice, they were so afraid they fell flat on the ground.
text: Se bakasebi itsyǝ bamfo lɛnɔ ɔkɔɛ ǝmfoo, sikpi ǝmuǝ mǝ tinti nya béfi ǝnǝmi béfusǝ kasɔ.
translated: When the hunter goes to the farm, he does not go to play, but rather to catch animals to bring home and sell.

ref: He will place the sheep on his right and the goats on his left.
text: Wuuńyǝsǝ betidi sɛɛ lǝ wǝ kodiani, nya wuutǝ mba ni betidi bua ńyǝ lǝ wǝ kǝmǝni.
translated: The hunter went to the farm, and when he got there, he saw that the hunter had already gone to the farm to harvest the crops.

ref: Every tree producing bad fruit will be chopped down and burned.
text: Kuyi saa ko laannwɔ ebibi sɛɛ, baala ko kefi kakpe lǝ ute.
translated: The dog is very fierce, if you don't give it food it will bite

ref: But the gate to life is very narrow. The road that leads there is so hard to follow that only a few people find it. A Tree and Its Fruit (Luke
text: Fɛɛ nkpǝ eto kusu afɛ, nya ko bɔyɛ kesu mfó kpe ale, nya betidi esiani hã lǝǝnyǝ ko. Kuyi Saa Ku Ko Ebibi.
translated: When we were young, we didn't know the world, now we know the world, and we know the things that are in it. The name of our town is Saa Ku Ko Ebibi.

ref: So the Son of Man is Lord over the Sabbath.”
text: Nyaso mɔ, Utidi Eto Ubii, mɔ ke ni Ɔnwɛditsyǝyi eto Onamute.”
translated: My father, Utidi Eto Ubii, says that the Onamute is the descendant of Ɔnwɛditsyǝyi.

ref: But you let people get by without helping their parents when they should. You let them say that what they have has been offered to God.
text: Fɛɛ mi áte biǝ utidi nwǝ lesatsyuǝ nte nkǝ woofo ni bufi kawunsǝko wǝ anto nye ambe fɛ ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, ‘Mfi lesa nǝmfo ntsyǝ kɛnyɛ miǝ muufi ni ntǝ Onanto.’
translated: They told me that when the moon is full, the buffalo will come out of the bush and play with the elephant, and then I will say, 'Let us go and meet our uncle in Onanto.'

ref: The crowds were surprised to hear what Jesus was teaching. The Most Important Commandment (Mark
text: Se sitiditu siamǝ lɛnɔ asa yamfoo, íyifo mǝ yanii, lǝ wǝ asatete ǝsuǝ. Yesu Édi Etiki Lǝ Kɔfi Ko Nso Bofe Ǝsuǝ.
translated: When the righteous are in authority, the people rejoice, but when the wicked are in power, the people groan. Jesus is the King of kings and the Lord of lords.

ref: Some Pharisees and several teachers of the Law of Moses from Jerusalem came and gathered around Jesus.
text: Farisifɔ ku afi eto bateasa ba dítsyi Yerusalem ǝbǝ bátsyilama Yesu.
translated: Many people went up to Jerusalem before the Passover to cleanse themselves

ref: After the wise men had gone, an angel from the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream and said, “Get up! Hurry and take the child and his mother to Egypt! Stay there until I tell you to return, because Herod is looking for the child and wants to kill him.”
text: Se babesibe bamǝ dísinkli bédu eto kamaa, Onanto eto ɔtɔnkpe utsyuǝ ǝlǝkǝ ǝsuǝ óte Yosef lǝ kola kǝmiǝ. Nya ɔ́tɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Oka Herodes ebutǝ nkǝ bati dikpefi nǝmǝ kama lǝ balo wǝ. Nyaso taka, lǝ etsyetsyiko dikpefi nǝmǝ ku ni ambe esuko Egipte eto kasɔ. Bisiǝ mfó isu ese lefe nǝ mɔɔtɛyi fǝ minkǝ bisinkli bibǝ.”
translated: Now when Jesus was born in Bethlehem of Judea in the days of King Herod, behold, wise men from the East came to Jerusalem, saying, “Where is he who has been born king of the Jews? For we saw his star when it rose and have come to worship him.” When Herod the king heard this, he was troubled, and all Jerusalem with him; and assembling all the chief priests and scribes of the people, he inquired of them where the Christ was to be born.

ref: and teach them to do everything I have told you. I will be with you always, even until the end of the world.
text: nya bete mǝ bǝ betsyue lesa saa nǝ mínte mi koto. Nya lǝ sitintii, mɔɔkɛ ku mi lefe saa, isu ese lǝ kawunsiǝ eto kalosǝkɔ.”
translated: When I came, my child was sick, and I went to the hospital. I was told that the child would not survive, and that is why I am crying.

ref: They left in a boat for a place where they could be alone.
text: Nyaso bésu bédufǝ lǝ ɔklɔ lǝ besu kaka betidi lǝmbǝ.
translated: The child is crying because of the pain in his stomach.

ref: Then about that time Jesus shouted, “Eli, Eli, lema sabachthani?” which means, “My God, my God, why have you deserted me?”
text: Fe abombo ǝtsyǝ etii, Yesu áfa bembembe ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Eli, Eli, lama sabaktani?” Nyamfo eto kasɔ ni, “Mɔ Onanto, mɔ Onanto! Beso fáyani mɛ fǝtsyǝ?”
translated: And about the ninth hour, Jesus cried out with a loud voice, “Eli, Eli, lama sabachthani?” which means, “My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?”

ref: After Jesus had sent the crowds away, he got into a boat and sailed across the lake. He came to shore near the town of Magadan.
text: Se Yesu dítsyǝ betidi bamǝ kusu olosǝǝ, údufǝ lǝ ɔklɔ nya údu úsu Magadan eto lekpake eti.
translated: And Jesus told them a parable, to the effect that they ought always to pray and not lose heart, he said there was a judge in a certain city who did not fear God or respect man

ref: so the disciples went over to him and woke him up. They said, “Lord, wake up! Save us before we drown!”
text: Bakasebi bamǝ ésu básanklisǝ wǝ nya bátɛyi wǝ bǝnkǝ, “Bonamute, lǝkǝ bo! Boǝbǝ bukpǝ lo.”
translated: The hunters went to the farm and met a certain woman, “Good morning, sir! Come and sit down.”
